$comment: D-AS-REGALLOC-SPILL-RELOAD-SCRATCH (c75, 2026-07-01): a function under GENERAL register pressure (peak simultaneous live values > the target's allocatable GPR count) must still be able to RELOAD a spilled vreg. The linear-scan allocator (lir_regalloc.cpp) reserved nothing for spill reloads, and the rewriter (lir_rewrite.cpp pickScratchRegs) built its scratch pool function-GLOBALLY (registers assigned to no range anywhere) — so a function that assigns all 15 x86_64 GPRs left the reload path with an EMPTY scratch pool -> error[L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc]. This was the frontier the post-c74 re-probe reached: sqlite3.c compiled 12m57s through the whole 8 MB amalgamation, then func 1033 (a spilled `arg`, isCall=0 — general pressure, NOT a wide call) hit this. FIX: buildFreeLists reserves K caller-saved registers per class as guaranteed spill-reload scratch, K = the function's max single-instruction register-reload demand (computeReloadReserve — derived per-target from the actual operand shapes, never a hardcoded count); the reserved registers flow to pickScratchRegs's pool automatically since they stay unassigned + allocatable. WITNESS: f has 15 int params, each used in TWO inline sums; while the second sum evaluates, the first sum's result (1 vreg) coexists with all 15 still-live param vregs -> MAXLIVE=16 > 15 GPRs -> spill -> (pre-fix) empty-pool exhaustion. main calls f with 15 args (a call that FITS the register file — no wide-call operand explosion, the separate deferred D-AS-REGALLOC-WIDE-CALL-OPERAND-COUNT). arm64 (~30 GPRs) never spills here (compiles trivially, verifies the value). RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the reservation -> error[L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc] 'function 1 exhausted the per-class scratch pool' on x86_64 (WITNESSED). => 42.
